title: invairiant.config.yml
description: Per-project configuration for invAIriant audits. Defaults implement the protocol's hard rules; note that allow_findings_without_evidence cannot be set to true — that would not be an invAIriant audit.

type.description: Free-form project type used for lens selection guidance. Common values: minimal-webapp, product-saas, infra-service, data-platform, ai-agent-system, distributed-system, p2p-network, embedded.

canonical_docs.description: Docs treated as canon for doc/code-contradiction evidence: README, ARCHITECTURE, THREAT_MODEL, ADRs. Divergence between these and code is auditable drift.

mandatory_lenses.description: Lens ids scored in every full-scale audit of this project. Keep to 4-6; a small default set beats a tribunal.

optional_lens_packs.description: Packs available for event-triggered or deep audits: systems, implementation, correctness, security-safety, ai-generated-code, domain packs.

critical_lenses.description: Lenses subject to the stricter critical-domain threshold (default: security-threat, privacy-knowledge-minimization, plus any domain lens guarding the project's core risk).

risk_assets.description: What the project must protect, e.g. credentials, user data, availability, operator safety. Severity classification references these.

require_file_or_diff_reference.description: If true, every finding needs at least one file_lines or diff_hunk item unless its evidence is a test failure, CI run, log, or incident.

allow_findings_without_evidence.description: Hard rule: no evidence, no finding. This key exists only to make the rule visible in every config.

low_score_threshold.description: Mandatory-lens score below this produces a finding of at least S2.

critical_domain_threshold.description: Score below this on a critical lens, tied to a concrete user/operational risk, produces S0 unless explicitly justified otherwise.

id_prefix.description: Finding id prefix, e.g. INV -> INV-001.

escalates_to.description: Severity when the stated escalation condition holds.

named_categories.description: Project-specific named finding categories extending the generic registry in docs/severity-model.md.

evidence_adapters.description: External tools/skills whose outputs are ingested as evidence (never directly as findings): e.g. semgrep, codeql, security-review, code-review, tech-debt-tracker, pytest, ci.
